note: Config for the Actionable Market Brief cockpit: machine-readable universe + deep-link map + update windows + thresholds + an agent-maintained macro-events list + a standing global/geopolitical backdrop. All analytics are computed LIVE from the shared rlData cache; nothing here is a stored analytic. STRUCTURE FIRST, NOISE LAST: the brief anchors every read in the larger picture (20/50/200 MA, 126/252d trend, macro cycle, global tensions, what's priced in) and never flips a call on a single-window intraday micro-delta (see notes/market-brief.md §6c). track.groups holds the thematic mega-cap / semis baskets (Mag 7 proxied by MAGS, semis by SOXX) — Tier-A computes the group ETF read + each member + breadth, and the agent elevates the NOTABLE members per run (see §7a). The agent follows notes/market-brief.md. Educational only — not investment advice.

windows.label: Pre-market
windows.focus: overnight/pre-market gaps, futures, Asia/Europe, overnight news; today's events + option-implied moves; gap-fill odds; levels to watch at the open

windows.label: Morning
windows.focus: did the open hold/reject key levels (gamma flip, 20/50/200 MA); morning trend vs the pre-market thesis; rotation confirmation; intraday breadth

windows.label: Pre-close
windows.focus: positioning into the close; gamma pin/magnet; MOC imbalance tells; hold vs hedge overnight; tomorrow's pre-market events

windows.label: After-hours
windows.focus: after-hours earnings reactions; regime/rotation deltas vs the prior close; what changed today + expected follow-through; overnight risk

groups.label: Magnificent 7
groups.desc: The seven mega-caps that dominate index returns, proxied by the Roundhill MAGS ETF. The group read = leading/lagging vs SPY + the 20/50/200-day MA stack; breadth = how many of the 7 are individually bull-stacked. Notable/moving members are elevated per run (§7a). Members mirror the sector-lab MAG7 basket.

groups.label: Semiconductors
groups.desc: The semiconductor complex, proxied by the iShares SOXX ETF — the AI-capex 'build-it' side that leads/lags mega-cap software. Group read + breadth + notable-member elevation per run (§7a). The brief uses SOXX; the sector lab's own SEMIS basket keeps its SMH proxy.

dataSources.bars: yahoo v8 chart (adjusted) via direct->allorigins->codetabs proxies; twelvedata (shared rlApiKeys key, 8/min) fallback
dataSources.options: yahoo option chains per-expiry via public proxies (same mechanism as gamma/options labs); reuse rlData.options / optSnaps
dataSources.fearGreed: CNN fearandgreed/graphdata JSON via proxy (same free-proxy mechanism); consumed by rlg.js
dataSources.breadth: computed in-browser from sector/watchlist member bars (adv/dec, %>50d) — a proxy, not an exchange breadth feed
dataSources.flows: PROXY ONLY (no free real-time fund flows): OBV, $-volume trend, up/down-volume delta, sector adv/dec, relative volume, price-vs-VWAP. Always labeled 'flow proxy'.
dataSources.events: per-ticker earnings/ex-div from yahoo (rlData.events); macro econ calendar (CPI/FOMC/NFP/OPEX) agent-maintained in macroEvents[] below from public schedules
dataSources.globalBackdrop: standing larger-picture themes (monetary cycle, rates/supply, AI-capex cycle, energy, USD/liquidity, geopolitics, China, carry) agent-maintained in globalBackdrop[] below; LOW-CHURN but verify each run — read the CURRENT market impact, never carry a stale geopolitical/commodity claim as fact

macroEvents.note: June CPI 8:30am ET (BLS-confirmed 7/14, web-re-verified 7/11) — the marquee near-term macro catalyst, and it COLLIDES with a HEAVY Q2 big-bank open: JPMorgan + Wells Fargo + Bank of America + Citigroup + Goldman Sachs ALL report pre-open (~7am ET) the SAME morning (web-verified 7/11; Morgan Stanley follows 7/15), so the bank tape opens INTO the CPI number (XLF read-through). A hot print re-arms the hawkish-hold hike tail (~15-18%) into a tape whose 2-week RRG momentum is already cooling (XLK/SOXX Weakening ↓); oil fading to ~$72 (CARRIED/STALE) eases the headline tail. Verify consensus at release.

June PPI 8:30am ET (BLS-confirmed 7/15, web-re-verified 7/11) — PCE feed-through, confirms/fades CPI a day later; Morgan Stanley reports pre-open the same day (7/15, web-verified) as the bank tape continues

June retail sales 8:30am ET (Census — 7/16, web-re-verified 7/11 vs Census July-2026 schedule) — consumer-demand read landing the session before OPEX; weekly initial jobless claims hit the same 8:30am slot; control group feeds the Q2 GDP nowcast; XLY/XLP read-through
monthly options expiration — lands 1-3 days after the CPI/PPI/retail-sales cluster

FOMC decision (Jul 28-29 meeting), 2:00pm ET (announcement 7/29, press conf 2:30pm) — same day as MSFT print. A non-SEP meeting (no new dot plot). Path WEB-CORROBORATED 7/11 (CME FedWatch / Kalshi): ~79% HOLD, ~2% cut, ~15-18% HIKE tail; Fed funds 3.50-3.75% held FOUR straight meetings, NO cuts imminent / 2026 median dot rose, hawkish ('inflation remains elevated') — a hawkish-hold, NOT a cut path

globalBackdrop.note: Where in the CB cycle are we + what the curve PRICES (cut/hold/hike odds into the 7/28-29 FOMC) — the direction, not the next print. Agent-maintained standing theme; verify the priced path live each run.

Long-end Treasury supply + auction DEMAND as a duration-risk driver (7/8 10y stopped 4.58% at a soft ~2.6 cover; 30y $22B reopening 7/9). A tail re-pressures long-duration tech. Verify each auction live.

The AI-capex spend cycle is the market's dominant SECULAR driver (mega-cap capex guides, semis). A 'show-me' vs 'build-it' tape governs tech leadership beyond any single session. Deep-link ai-capex-strategy-lab; verify the current read.

Crude/energy trend doubles as the headline-CPI tail AND a geopolitical-risk gauge (a rolling-over XLE = crude fading = a cooler-CPI read-through). Verify WTI live — never carry a stale commodity level as fact.
USD trend, real yields and global liquidity as the risk-asset tide. A rising USD/real-yield combo tightens; falling loosens. Standing macro-cycle input; verify direction live.

Standing global-tension set — armed conflict / war-risk, trade & tariffs & sanctions, elections & policy shifts. Read the CURRENT market impact each run; NEVER fabricate breaking news — cite it or label STALE/estimate.
China growth + policy stimulus as a global-demand and commodity swing factor (materials/industrials/energy read-through). Standing theme; verify the live stance each run.
JPY-carry & cross-asset contagion tail — a sharp yen strengthening (narrowing US-JP rate differential) can force a high-beta / tech de-gross. Low-probability, high-impact standing tail; verify live.
